title: Congressional behavior: foundational
description: Test your knowledge on how Congress works!
topic_path: How do members of Congress represent the people?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the area that a particular legislator in the House represents?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**How many senators are distributed to each state?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It depends on the state's population size
It depends on the state's geographic size

assessment_items.question_content:
**How long is a term for members of the House of Representatives?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Why did the Framers want to create a bicameral legislature?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to balance congressional power between big states and small states
to speed up the lawmaking process
to minimize the control one political party has in the legislature
to ensure that Congress is the most powerful branch of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**What political conflict causes a divided government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president's political party is opposite from the party that controls Congress.
The Senate and the House refuse to agree on what should be in a new law.
The Senate refuses to end debate on a bill even though the House version has passed.
The two political parties refuse to work together to pass legislation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who is responsible for drawing congressional districts every ten years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the term for when one party redraws legislative districts in order to gain an advantage over the other party?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What was the decision in *Baker v. Carr* (1962)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The courts have some jurisdiction over legislative redistricting disputes.
The Supreme Court can decide if the actions of the other branches of government are unconstitutional.
Redistricting based on race must be held to a standard of strict scrutiny.
The Congress cannot use the necessary and proper clause to justify interfering with a state's own laws.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following saying is a principle of the equal protection clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What Supreme Court case established that race cannot be a main factor for determining voting districts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

What role is a politician taking on when they vote exactly as their constituent request?

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is a word for an elected official who votes with their conscience?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following describes the politico model of representation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They vote based on how much their constituents care about an issue and what the voters want.
They only vote how they think the people in their home state or district want them to vote.
They are loyal party members who always vote with their political party dictates.
They decide how to vote based on their own personal beliefs and judgments.

assessment_items.question_content:
Congress member Sandy Cheeks represents a district with a huge fishing industry. Her constituents ask her to oppose an environmental regulation bill that will increase the cost of running their fisheries. So, she votes against the bill. 

**What model of representation is Representative Cheeks displaying?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Senator Krabs received thousands of letters and phone calls asking him to support a bill. The bill orders restaurants to list the ingredients in all of their products on their menu boards. Senator Krabs believes that the bill will cause valuable fast food restaurants in his district to go out of business, so he votes against the bill. 

**What model of representation is Senator Krabs portraying?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the name for a situation in which the president and Congress fail to make progress on major political issues?
 **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What does it mean for there to be a divided government?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The majority party in Congress is not the party that the president belongs to.
The party that controls the Senate is different than the majority party in the House of Representatives.
Politicians only act in alignment with their party and refuse to compromise with the other side.
Politicians continue to do government work even though their successors have already been elected.

assessment_items.question_content:
A healthcare bill is introduced in the Senate. Every Republican votes in favor of the bill and every Democrat votes against it. 

**What is this an example of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In November, a new Republican is elected to a seat in the Senate that a Democrat currently holds. The Democrat will be at a congressional session in December before the new senator enters office. 

**Which of the following describes this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is gerrymandering?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a redistricting tactic that allows one part to gain an unfair advantage over the other party
a process of redistributing representatives across new districts after the census
a strategy to delay a vote in the Senate by debating a bill, sometimes to an excess degree
an active effort to gain popularity with voters in a particular district before an election
